result.summary: Cloud Identity exposes a REST API at `cloudidentity.googleapis.com` and is also accessible through `gcloud identity` CLI commands; both use Google-authenticated credentials.
result.description: Google Cloud Identity is Google's identity and endpoint-management service for managing groups, devices, user invitations, and related policies in an organization. It is used by administrators to manage workforce identity resources tied to Google Cloud and Google Workspace environments.

google_oauth_access_token.label: Google OAuth 2.0 access token
google_oauth_access_token.setup: Create or select a Google Cloud project in the [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/), enable the Cloud Identity API, then create OAuth 2.0 client credentials from [APIs & Services → Credentials](https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials) or use a service account for server-to-server access. The Cloud Identity setup guides say to enable the API and, for service accounts, grant domain-wide delegation/admin privileges as needed for your organization. Use the resulting OAuth 2.0 bearer access token when calling `cloudidentity.googleapis.com`.

gcloud_login.label: Google Cloud CLI login
gcloud_login.setup: Install the [Google Cloud CLI](https://cloud.google.com/sdk/docs/install), then authenticate with [`gcloud auth login`](https://cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/auth/login) or another supported `gcloud auth` flow. The `gcloud identity` commands use your active gcloud credentials to call the Cloud Identity API.
